name: LinkedIn
philosophy: LinkedIn's web interface is the professional network's chrome — LinkedIn Blue (#0a66c2) as the chromatic anchor, a clean off-white canvas (#f3f2ef), and a humanist sans (LinkedIn's Brand Sans / Source Sans Pro) running the chrome. The atmosphere is white-collar formal, card-based, and feed-dominant; the design reads as 'newspaper of work' rather than social-media-playful.

design_language.motion_signature: Functional feed-scroll motion — minimal animation, 150ms hover transitions, content lazy-loads as you scroll. Reactions (like, celebrate, support) animate with a small spring scale on press. No spring overshoot elsewhere.

trademark_signals: LinkedIn Blue (#0a66c2) — the primary chromatic asset on CTAs, links, and the wordmark
Off-white canvas (#f3f2ef) — a tinted background that distinguishes the feed from pure white, making cards stand out
Pill-shaped 'Connect' / 'Follow' buttons in LinkedIn Blue — the signature CTA shape
Card-based feed grammar — every post, every job, every announcement sits in a rounded card on the tinted canvas
Reaction picker — 6 colored emoji-style reactions (Like blue, Celebrate yellow, Support purple, Love red, Insightful amber, Funny teal)
ai_slop_avoided: Don't replace the off-white canvas with pure white — the tint is what makes cards visually 'lift'
Don't square the pill CTAs — full-radius is the brand's CTA shape
Don't replace LinkedIn Blue with a brighter consumer-blue — the specific corporate-leaning blue is part of the recognition
Don't animate the feed cards on scroll — the brand reads as serious workplace, not TikTok
